_meta.cycle_disposition_reason: Weekly research bundle contains multiple new-this-cycle developments across Russia-Ukraine (I2 deadliest civilian strike wave, I5 ceasefire collapse, I1 NATO threats, I4 sanctions contradictions), Sudan (I2 multi-front expansion, I4 economic warfare, I6 displacement surge), and Iran (I1 Houthi warnings, I2 proxy activation). Reasoner confirms trajectory changes warranted for Sudan, Russia-Ukraine, Israel-Lebanon, and Korea. Material change threshold clearly met.

lead_signal.headline: Russia conducted its deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026 in the days preceding the Trump-brokered 9-11 May Victory Day ceasefire, killing at least 73 civilians and injuring over 400 across eastern Ukrainian population centres.
lead_signal.body: Between 4 and 8 May 2026, Russian forces executed the deadliest wave of strikes on civilians recorded in 2026, killing at least 73 civilians and injuring over 400 across Zaporizhia, Kramatorsk, Dnipro, Merefa, and Sumy. ACLED characterises the pattern as heightened civilian targeting around ceasefires, mirroring prior ceasefire-adjacent escalation cycles. The strikes focused on population centres close to the frontline, indicating a deliberate pre-ceasefire pressure campaign rather than tactical military necessity. Russia resumed strikes on Ukrainian cities immediately after the ceasefire window closed, consistent with the pattern following the Orthodox Easter ceasefire in late April.

module_0.title: Russia executes deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026 ahead of Victory Day ceasefire
module_0.body: Between 4 and 8 May 2026, Russian forces conducted the deadliest wave of strikes on civilians recorded in 2026, killing at least 73 civilians and injuring over 400 across Zaporizhia, Kramatorsk, Dnipro, Merefa, and Sumy. ACLED characterises the pattern as heightened civilian targeting around ceasefires, mirroring prior ceasefire-adjacent escalation cycles. The strikes focused on population centres close to the frontline, indicating a deliberate pre-ceasefire pressure campaign rather than tactical military necessity. Russia resumed strikes on Ukrainian cities immediately after the ceasefire window closed, consistent with the pattern following the Orthodox Easter ceasefire in late April. This represents a structural escalation in civilian harm velocity tied to diplomatic windows, not episodic violence.

mainstream.headline: Russia executes deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026 ahead of Victory Day ceasefire
mainstream.why_it_matters: The deliberate timing of the deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026 immediately before a US-brokered ceasefire demonstrates a structural pattern of Russian coercive signalling through civilian harm. ACLED documentation of 73 killed and over 400 injured across five population centres in four days represents a significant escalation in civilian targeting velocity. The pattern of ceasefire-adjacent escalation is now in its second documented cycle (Easter + Victory Day), approaching structural threshold. This signals that Russian strategy treats diplomatic windows not as de-escalation opportunities but as leverage points for maximising civilian pressure before temporary pauses.
mainstream.body: The deliberate timing of the deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026 immediately before a US-brokered ceasefire demonstrates a structural pattern of Russian coercive signalling through civilian harm. ACLED documentation of 73 killed and over 400 injured across five population centres in four days represents a significant escalation in civilian targeting velocity. The pattern of ceasefire-adjacent escalation is now in its second documented cycle (Easter + Victory Day), approaching structural threshold. This signals that Russian strategy treats diplomatic windows not as de-escalation opportunities but as leverage points for maximising civilian pressure before temporary pauses.
mainstream.asymmetric: The ceasefire-adjacent escalation pattern is underweighted in mainstream coverage, which tends to frame ceasefires as de-escalation signals. The structural use of civilian harm as a pre-ceasefire bargaining chip indicates that future US-brokered pauses may predictably trigger rather than prevent civilian casualty spikes.

mainstream.headline: Trump-brokered Victory Day ceasefire collapses immediately; Russia resumes strikes
mainstream.why_it_matters: The three-day ceasefire coinciding with Russia's Victory Day (9-11 May) produced no structural diplomatic progress and no framework for sustained negotiations. Russia resumed strikes on Ukrainian cities immediately after the ceasefire window closed, consistent with the pattern following the Orthodox Easter ceasefire in late April. The ceasefire carries reduced I5 weight due to US diplomatic de-weighting: unilateral US-brokered arrangements without multilateral enforcement mechanisms have proven structurally fragile. This is the second consecutive US-brokered ceasefire to collapse within 24 hours of expiry, signalling that bilateral US-Russia arrangements lack the institutional architecture to sustain de-escalation.
mainstream.body: The three-day ceasefire coinciding with Russia's Victory Day (9-11 May) produced no structural diplomatic progress and no framework for sustained negotiations. Russia resumed strikes on Ukrainian cities immediately after the ceasefire window closed, consistent with the pattern following the Orthodox Easter ceasefire in late April. The ceasefire carries reduced I5 weight due to US diplomatic de-weighting: unilateral US-brokered arrangements without multilateral enforcement mechanisms have proven structurally fragile. This is the second consecutive US-brokered ceasefire to collapse within 24 hours of expiry, signalling that bilateral US-Russia arrangements lack the institutional architecture to sustain de-escalation.
mainstream.asymmetric: The structural fragility of US-brokered ceasefires without European or UN involvement is underweighted in coverage that treats any ceasefire announcement as a positive I5 signal. The pattern of immediate post-ceasefire resumption indicates that these pauses function as operational breathing space for Russia rather than genuine diplomatic openings.

mainstream.headline: Sudan SAF launches multi-front expansion: Blue Nile, Kordofan airstrikes, gold mine targeting
mainstream.why_it_matters: Sudan Armed Forces (SAF) opened a new front in Blue Nile state with the capture of Damazin on 28 April, while simultaneously conducting manned airstrikes in Kordofan and targeting RSF-controlled gold mines as economic warfare. This represents a structural shift from the year-long stalemate to multi-domain offensive operations. The Blue Nile expansion threatens to partition Sudan along north-south lines, while gold mine strikes introduce deliberate revenue degradation strategy against RSF. ACLED and ICG both flag this as the most significant operational tempo increase in year four of the war, with implications for regional spillover into Chad and South Sudan.
mainstream.body: Sudan Armed Forces (SAF) opened a new front in Blue Nile state with the capture of Damazin on 28 April, while simultaneously conducting manned airstrikes in Kordofan and targeting RSF-controlled gold mines as economic warfare. This represents a structural shift from the year-long stalemate to multi-domain offensive operations. The Blue Nile expansion threatens to partition Sudan along north-south lines, while gold mine strikes introduce deliberate revenue degradation strategy against RSF. ACLED and ICG both flag this as the most significant operational tempo increase in year four of the war, with implications for regional spillover into Chad and South Sudan.
mainstream.asymmetric: The introduction of economic warfare targeting (gold mines) signals a shift from purely kinetic to hybrid strategy, yet this dimension is absent from most coverage focused on territorial gains. The Blue Nile front opens a potential north-south partition scenario that could create a permanent failed-state corridor from Khartoum to the Ethiopian border.

underweighted.headline: Shoigu warns Finland and Baltic states over alleged Ukrainian use of their airspace for strikes on Russia
underweighted.why_it_matters: Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu on 16 April warned Finland and Baltic countries that permitting Ukraine to use their airspace for operations on Russian soil would make them accomplices to aggression, potentially giving Moscow grounds to invoke Article 51 of the UN Charter on the right to self-defence. Separately, Medvedev on 15 April published a list of European drone manufacturers designated as potential targets for Russian Armed Forces. Both statements represent escalatory rhetorical posturing toward NATO member states. While these fall within the established pattern of Russian coercive signalling, the explicit invocation of Article 51 against NATO members is a novel escalation in legal framing.
underweighted.body: Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu on 16 April warned Finland and Baltic countries that permitting Ukraine to use their airspace for operations on Russian soil would make them accomplices to aggression, potentially giving Moscow grounds to invoke Article 51 of the UN Charter on the right to self-defence. Separately, Medvedev on 15 April published a list of European drone manufacturers designated as potential targets for Russian Armed Forces. Both statements represent escalatory rhetorical posturing toward NATO member states. While these fall within the established pattern of Russian coercive signalling, the explicit invocation of Article 51 against NATO members is a novel escalation in legal framing.
underweighted.asymmetric: The Article 51 framing is underweighted in coverage that treats this as routine Russian bluster. Invoking the UN Charter self-defence provision against NATO members creates a legal predicate for future kinetic action that could be framed as defensive rather than offensive. This is a structural shift in Russian legal positioning, not episodic rhetoric.

underweighted.headline: Iran presses Houthis for renewed Red Sea shipping attacks if US escalates
underweighted.why_it_matters: Iran is reportedly urging Houthis to resume attacks on Red Sea shipping if the US escalates against Iran, despite internal Houthi divisions. This proxy activation risk threatens 10 percent of global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb and compounds the existing Strait of Hormuz closure. The Houthis issued a military intervention warning on 21 April stating they will intervene if regional countries ally with the US or Israel against Iran, if the Red Sea is used for hostile operations, or if escalation against Iran or the Axis of Resistance continues. This creates a structural linkage between Iran escalation and Red Sea chokepoint disruption that is absent from most Iran coverage.
underweighted.body: Iran is reportedly urging Houthis to resume attacks on Red Sea shipping if the US escalates against Iran, despite internal Houthi divisions. This proxy activation risk threatens 10 percent of global seaborne trade via Bab al-Mandeb and compounds the existing Strait of Hormuz closure. The Houthis issued a military intervention warning on 21 April stating they will intervene if regional countries ally with the US or Israel against Iran, if the Red Sea is used for hostile operations, or if escalation against Iran or the Axis of Resistance continues. This creates a structural linkage between Iran escalation and Red Sea chokepoint disruption that is absent from most Iran coverage.
underweighted.asymmetric: The dual-chokepoint risk (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb) is underweighted in coverage that treats the Iran conflict and Yemen as separate theatres. Simultaneous closure of both straits would affect approximately 30 percent of global seaborne oil trade and 10 percent of all maritime commerce, creating a systemic energy and commodities shock.

treaty_status.key_development: No change this week. Russia suspended participation February 2023. No successor negotiations active.
treaty_status.asymmetric: Absence of strategic arms control framework between US and Russia leaves no verification mechanism for nuclear posture changes. Russia November 2024 doctrine shift (lowering nuclear threshold) occurred in a verification vacuum.

treaty_status.key_development: No change this week. DPRK nuclear acceleration continues (7 tests 2026 to April). Iran nuclear programme at impasse.
treaty_status.asymmetric: NPT compliance verification relies on IAEA safeguards, which are absent for DPRK (withdrew 2003) and contested for Iran. Treaty architecture under structural stress from non-compliance without enforcement.

doctrine_shifts.description: Russia November 2024 lowered nuclear threshold to permit nuclear response to conventional attack threatening territorial integrity. Doctrine shift is Confirmed and Persistent per FM-SCEM-03. No new developments this week.
doctrine_shifts.asymmetric: Doctrine shift occurred in New START suspension vacuum, meaning no external verification of Russian nuclear posture changes. Subsequent Medvedev or MFA rhetoric is episodic, not structural.

capability_developments.development: DPRK nuclear acceleration ordered; 7 tests 2026 to April
capability_developments.signal: REPORTED — satellite analysis is T2 for DPRK; Confirmed confidence rarely achievable per standing OSINT gap
capability_developments.analysis: DPRK nuclear acceleration order combined with 7 tests in four months represents above-baseline I3 activity. US asset redeployment to Middle East reduces deterrence posture on Korean Peninsula, creating permissive environment for DPRK testing surge. Warrants Worsening trajectory per reasoner analysis.

headline_developments: Russia conducted deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026 (73 killed, 400+ injured) in four days preceding Victory Day ceasefire
Trump-brokered 9-11 May ceasefire collapsed immediately; Russia resumed strikes within 24 hours
Shoigu warned Finland and Baltic states over alleged Ukrainian use of their airspace, invoking Article 51 of UN Charter
EU adopted 20th sanctions package; US extended Russian oil sanctions waiver for 30 days, creating contradictory Western posture
theatres.deterrence_to_escalation_gap: Ceasefire-adjacent escalation pattern (Easter + Victory Day) demonstrates that Russian strategy treats diplomatic windows as leverage points for maximising civilian pressure, not de-escalation opportunities. US-brokered ceasefires without multilateral enforcement collapse within 24 hours, indicating structural fragility. Deterrence gap: NATO has no response framework for ceasefire-adjacent civilian harm spikes.
theatres.stealth_flag: The structural use of civilian harm as a pre-ceasefire bargaining chip is underweighted in coverage that treats ceasefires as de-escalation signals. The pattern is now in its second documented cycle, approaching structural threshold.
theatres.asymmetric: The Article 51 framing against NATO members creates a legal predicate for future kinetic action that could be framed as defensive rather than offensive. This is a structural shift in Russian legal positioning, not episodic rhetoric.

headline_developments: SAF opened new front in Blue Nile state with capture of Damazin on 28 April
SAF conducted manned airstrikes in Kordofan and targeted RSF-controlled gold mines as economic warfare
Displacement surged to 4 million+ refugees with new Blue Nile and Kordofan waves
Quad mediation (US, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt) collapsed; no active diplomatic framework
theatres.deterrence_to_escalation_gap: Multi-front expansion (Blue Nile + Kordofan + gold mine targeting) represents structural shift from year-long stalemate to multi-domain offensive operations. No functioning diplomatic framework after Quad mediation collapse. Deterrence gap: regional actors (Chad, South Sudan, Ethiopia) have no coordinated response to spillover risk.
theatres.stealth_flag: The introduction of economic warfare targeting (gold mines) signals a shift from purely kinetic to hybrid strategy, yet this dimension is absent from most coverage focused on territorial gains.
theatres.asymmetric: The Blue Nile front opens a potential north-south partition scenario that could create a permanent failed-state corridor from Khartoum to the Ethiopian border. Gold mine strikes aim to degrade RSF revenue streams via UAE networks, potentially reducing illicit gold flows.

headline_developments: Ceasefire holding nominally but daily violations continue
Hamas rejected disarmament; Phase 2 negotiations stalled
No new weekly data; F4 flag on I5 notes fragile ceasefire
theatres.deterrence_to_escalation_gap: Ceasefire is structurally fragile with daily violations and no Phase 2 framework. Hamas rejection of disarmament means no pathway to sustainable settlement. Deterrence gap: no enforcement mechanism for ceasefire violations.
theatres.stealth_flag: The fragility of the ceasefire is underweighted in coverage that treats any ceasefire as a positive I5 signal. Daily violations indicate the ceasefire is nominal only.
theatres.asymmetric: The absence of a Phase 2 framework means the ceasefire is a temporary pause, not a pathway to resolution. Hamas rejection of disarmament signals no structural change in posture.

Israeli strikes intensified across southern Lebanon to Litani River on 21 April
I6 displacement at RED threshold; new evacuation orders issued

theatres.stealth_flag: The ceasefire unraveling is underweighted in coverage that treats the November 2024 ceasefire as holding. Range extension toward Litani may signal ground preparation.
theatres.asymmetric: Israeli strikes expanding to Litani River suggest potential ground operation preparation. I6 displacement at RED threshold indicates humanitarian catastrophe risk.

theatres.asymmetric: SCEF represents first credible multi-ethnic resistance coordination since 2021 coup. If sustained, could shift balance against junta.

european.headline: Shoigu warns Finland and Baltic states over alleged Ukrainian use of their airspace, invoking Article 51 of UN Charter

nato_developments.item: Shoigu Article 51 warning to Finland and Baltic states
nato_developments.summary: Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu on 16 April warned Finland and Baltic countries that permitting Ukraine to use their airspace for operations on Russian soil would make them accomplices to aggression, potentially giving Moscow grounds to invoke Article 51 of the UN Charter on the right to self-defence. This represents a novel escalation in legal framing, creating a legal predicate for future kinetic action against NATO members that could be framed as defensive rather than offensive.
nato_developments.asymmetric: The Article 51 framing is underweighted in coverage that treats this as routine Russian bluster. Invoking the UN Charter self-defence provision against NATO members creates a structural shift in Russian legal positioning, not episodic rhetoric.

nato_developments.item: Medvedev publishes list of European drone manufacturers as potential Russian targets
nato_developments.summary: Dmitry Medvedev on 15 April published a list of European drone manufacturers designated as potential targets for Russian Armed Forces. This represents a hybrid FIMI and coercion operation, explicitly threatening civilian industrial targets in NATO member states. The publication creates a legal and operational predicate for future strikes on European soil framed as counter-force operations.
nato_developments.asymmetric: The publication of a target list is a hybrid warfare operation combining information warfare (coercion) with operational preparation (target designation). This is underweighted in coverage that treats it as rhetoric only.

eu_security_developments.item: EU adopts 20th sanctions package against Russia
eu_security_developments.summary: The EU adopted its 20th package of sanctions against Russia on 23 April 2026. Simultaneously, Washington extended sanctions relief on Russian oil for an additional 30 days after the previous waiver expired on 11 April, creating a contradictory Western posture. This undermines EU sanctions coherence and signals transatlantic divergence on economic coercion strategy.

european.ukraine_russia_status: Russia conducted its deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026 (73 killed, 400+ injured) in the four days preceding the Trump-brokered Victory Day ceasefire, then resumed strikes within 24 hours of the ceasefire expiring. The ceasefire-adjacent escalation pattern is now in its second documented cycle (Easter + Victory Day), approaching structural threshold. Shoigu warned Finland and Baltic states over alleged Ukrainian use of their airspace, invoking Article 51 of the UN Charter. The EU adopted its 20th sanctions package while the US extended Russian oil sanctions relief for 30 days, creating contradictory Western posture. Intensity: I3 Worsening.
european.balkan_watch: No new developments this week. Western Balkans remain at I1 baseline with no escalation signals.
european.baltic_nordic_watch: Shoigu Article 51 warning to Finland and Baltic states represents novel escalation in legal framing against NATO members. Medvedev published list of European drone manufacturers as potential Russian targets. No kinetic incidents reported, but rhetorical escalation creates legal predicate for future action. Intensity: I1 elevated.

indo_pacific.taiwan_strait: Two major PLA exercises in 2025; territorial water incursions normalised. No new developments this week. Latent levels at structural baseline. US commitment to Taiwan defence unclear under current administration. Intensity: I1 Stable.
indo_pacific.korean_peninsula: DPRK nuclear acceleration ordered with 7 tests conducted from January to April 2026. US asset redeployment to Middle East reduces deterrence posture on Korean Peninsula, creating permissive environment for testing surge. I1/I2/I3 structurally elevated. Warrants Worsening trajectory per reasoner analysis. Intensity: I3 Worsening.

Multiple indicators met: I1, I2, I3 (nuclear signalling), I5 (ceasefire achieved). Recent kinetic activity supports dual-indicator threshold for inclusion. No new developments this week. Intensity: I1 Stable (if included).

programme_updates.update: Nuclear acceleration ordered; 7 tests conducted January to April 2026. Satellite analysis is T2 for DPRK; Confirmed confidence rarely achievable per standing OSINT gap.

vectors.name: Nuclear Threshold Pressure
vectors.headline: DPRK nuclear acceleration (7 tests 2026) and Russia November 2024 doctrine shift (lowered threshold) sustain elevated nuclear threshold pressure
vectors.summary: DPRK conducted 7 nuclear tests from January to April 2026, the highest testing velocity since 2017. Russia November 2024 doctrine shift lowered nuclear threshold to permit nuclear response to conventional attack threatening territorial integrity. Iran nuclear programme at impasse with no verification framework. No active arms control negotiations between major nuclear powers.
vectors.detail: The combination of DPRK testing acceleration, Russia doctrine shift in a New START suspension vacuum, and Iran nuclear impasse creates a structural nuclear threshold pressure environment. DPRK testing surge occurs in a permissive environment created by US asset redeployment to Middle East. Russia doctrine shift occurred without external verification, meaning no transparency on nuclear posture changes. Iran nuclear programme lacks IAEA safeguards verification. No functioning arms control architecture to manage threshold pressure.
vectors.asymmetric: The absence of arms control verification frameworks means nuclear threshold pressure is rising in a transparency vacuum. DPRK, Russia, and Iran nuclear postures are all contested or unverified, creating compounding uncertainty.

vectors.name: Alliance Cohesion Stress
vectors.headline: Transatlantic divergence on Russia sanctions (EU 20th package vs US oil waiver extension) and US-brokered ceasefires without European involvement signal alliance cohesion stress
vectors.summary: The EU adopted its 20th sanctions package against Russia on 23 April while the US extended Russian oil sanctions relief for 30 days, creating contradictory Western posture. US-brokered ceasefires in Ukraine (Easter, Victory Day) collapsed within 24 hours without European or UN involvement, signalling marginalisation of European diplomatic agency. Shoigu Article 51 warning to Finland and Baltic states creates legal predicate for future kinetic action against NATO members.
vectors.detail: Transatlantic divergence on economic coercion strategy undermines EU sanctions coherence. US unilateral ceasefire brokering without European involvement signals continued marginalisation of European diplomatic agency in Ukraine settlement process. The structural fragility of US-brokered ceasefires without multilateral enforcement demonstrates that bilateral US-Russia arrangements lack institutional architecture to sustain de-escalation. Shoigu Article 51 framing against NATO members creates legal predicate for future action that could fracture alliance consensus on Article 5 response.
vectors.asymmetric: The marginalisation of European diplomatic agency is underweighted in coverage that treats US-brokered ceasefires as positive signals. The structural fragility of these arrangements indicates that NATO cohesion on Ukraine is weakening, not strengthening.

vectors.name: Diplomatic Channel Attrition
vectors.headline: US-brokered ceasefires collapse within 24 hours; Iran nuclear talks at impasse; Sudan Quad mediation collapsed; no active multilateral frameworks
vectors.summary: US-brokered ceasefires in Ukraine (Easter, Victory Day) collapsed within 24 hours of expiry, demonstrating structural fragility of bilateral arrangements without multilateral enforcement. Iran nuclear talks at impasse with 27 April decouple proposal rejected. Sudan Quad mediation (US, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt) collapsed with no active diplomatic framework. Gaza ceasefire fragile with daily violations and no Phase 2 framework. Israel-Lebanon ceasefire unraveling.
vectors.detail: The pattern of immediate post-ceasefire resumption in Ukraine indicates that bilateral US-Russia arrangements function as operational breathing space for Russia rather than genuine diplomatic openings. Iran nuclear impasse with no verification framework leaves no pathway to de-escalation. Sudan Quad mediation collapse leaves no functioning diplomatic framework for year-four war. Gaza and Lebanon ceasefires are nominal only with no enforcement mechanisms. Diplomatic channel attrition is structural, not episodic.
vectors.asymmetric: The structural fragility of US-brokered arrangements is underweighted in coverage that treats any ceasefire announcement as a positive I5 signal. The absence of multilateral frameworks means no institutional architecture to sustain de-escalation across multiple theatres.

vectors.name: Hybrid Warfare Intensity
vectors.headline: Medvedev publishes European drone manufacturer target list; Houthi Red Sea intervention warnings; AQAP drone surge in Yemen
vectors.summary: Dmitry Medvedev on 15 April published a list of European drone manufacturers designated as potential targets for Russian Armed Forces, representing a hybrid FIMI and coercion operation. Houthis issued military intervention warning on 21 April threatening Red Sea chokepoint disruption if Iran attacked. AQAP political violence in Yemen hit highest level since November 2022 using drones and IEDs, signalling reassertion amid Houthi tensions.
vectors.detail: Medvedev target list publication is a hybrid warfare operation combining information warfare (coercion) with operational preparation (target designation), explicitly threatening civilian industrial targets in NATO member states. Houthi Red Sea warnings create dual-chokepoint risk (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb) threatening 30 percent of global seaborne oil trade. AQAP drone use signals possible external support and links to broader Iran proxy dynamics. Hybrid warfare intensity elevated across multiple theatres.
vectors.asymmetric: The hybrid nature of Medvedev target list publication is underweighted in coverage that treats it as rhetoric only. The operational preparation dimension (target designation) creates legal and operational predicate for future strikes on European soil.

vectors.name: Proxy Conflict Spillover
vectors.headline: Sudan Blue Nile front threatens Chad and South Sudan spillover; Houthi Red Sea activation risk; AQAP resurgence in Yemen
vectors.summary: Sudan SAF Blue Nile offensive threatens regional spillover into Chad and South Sudan. Houthis warned of Red Sea intervention if Iran attacked, creating dual-chokepoint risk. AQAP political violence surge in Yemen signals reassertion amid Houthi tensions. Iran pressing Houthis for renewed Red Sea shipping attacks if US escalates.
vectors.detail: Sudan Blue Nile front opens potential permanent failed-state corridor from Khartoum to Ethiopian border, threatening Chad and South Sudan stability. Houthi Red Sea activation combined with Hormuz closure would affect 30 percent of global seaborne oil trade and 10 percent of all maritime commerce. AQAP drone use signals possible external support, linking to broader Iran proxy dynamics. Proxy conflict spillover risk is structural across Sahel, Horn of Africa, and Red Sea theatres.
vectors.asymmetric: The dual-chokepoint risk (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb) is underweighted in coverage that treats Iran conflict and Yemen as separate theatres. Sudan Blue Nile spillover risk is absent from most coverage focused on territorial gains.

vectors.name: Crisis Management Capacity
vectors.headline: No functioning multilateral crisis management frameworks; US-brokered arrangements structurally fragile; regional mechanisms (ASEAN, AU, OSCE) ineffective
vectors.summary: US-brokered ceasefires collapse within 24 hours. Iran nuclear talks at impasse. Sudan Quad mediation collapsed. Gaza and Lebanon ceasefires nominal only. No active arms control negotiations. Regional mechanisms (ASEAN on Myanmar, AU on Sudan, OSCE on Ukraine) ineffective.
vectors.detail: The absence of functioning multilateral crisis management frameworks across all major theatres represents structural crisis management capacity deficit. US bilateral arrangements lack institutional architecture to sustain de-escalation. Regional mechanisms are either absent (Sudan Quad collapsed) or ineffective (ASEAN on Myanmar, AU on Sudan, OSCE on Ukraine). No active arms control negotiations between major powers means no institutional channels for nuclear crisis management. Crisis management capacity is at structural low.
vectors.asymmetric: The structural deficit in crisis management capacity is underweighted in coverage that treats discrete ceasefire announcements as progress. The absence of multilateral frameworks means no institutional resilience when bilateral arrangements fail.

items.title: Houthis warn of Red Sea intervention if Iran attacked; Iran presses for renewed shipping attacks
items.summary: Houthis issued military intervention warning on 21 April stating they will intervene if regional countries ally with US or Israel against Iran, if Red Sea is used for hostile operations, or if escalation against Iran or Axis of Resistance continues. Iran reportedly urging Houthis to resume attacks on Red Sea shipping if US escalates against Iran, despite internal Houthi divisions. This creates dual-chokepoint risk (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb) threatening 30 percent of global seaborne oil trade and 10 percent of all maritime commerce.
items.asymmetric: The dual-chokepoint risk is underweighted in coverage that treats Iran conflict and Yemen as separate theatres. Simultaneous closure of both straits would create systemic energy and commodities shock.

items.title: AQAP drone and IED surge vs STC forces in Yemen; highest violence since November 2022
items.summary: AQAP political violence hit highest level since November 2022 using drones and IEDs against STC affiliates in Abyan and Shawba. AQAP killed STC commander and troops. ACLED data indicates possible external support. Drone use signals AQAP reassertion amid Houthi tensions and links to broader Iran proxy dynamics.
items.asymmetric: AQAP resurgence is underweighted in coverage focused on Houthi-Saudi dynamics. Drone capability suggests external support, potentially linking to Iran proxy network.

module_9.title: Sanctions, Economic Coercion, and Legal Frameworks
module_9.digest_note: Transatlantic divergence on Russia sanctions undermines EU coherence; Sudan gold mine strikes introduce economic warfare dimension

new_developments.title: EU adopts 20th sanctions package against Russia; US extends oil waiver for 30 days
new_developments.summary: The EU adopted its 20th package of sanctions against Russia on 23 April 2026. Simultaneously, Washington extended sanctions relief on Russian oil for an additional 30 days after the previous waiver expired on 11 April, creating contradictory Western posture. This undermines EU sanctions coherence and signals transatlantic divergence on economic coercion strategy. Ukraine has been targeting Russian oil infrastructure to offset Russia revenue gains from elevated energy prices driven by Iran conflict.
new_developments.enforcement: EU enforcement mechanisms active; US waiver creates enforcement gap
new_developments.asymmetric: The contradictory posture (EU sanctions vs US waiver) is underweighted in coverage that treats each development separately. The divergence signals weakening transatlantic coordination on economic coercion.

new_developments.title: Sudan SAF targets RSF-controlled gold mines as economic warfare
new_developments.summary: Sudan Armed Forces conducted manned airstrikes on RSF-controlled gold mines in Kordofan in late April 2026, introducing deliberate economic warfare targeting to degrade RSF revenue streams. Gold mine strikes aim to reduce illicit gold flows via UAE networks. This represents a shift from purely kinetic to hybrid strategy in year-four war.
new_developments.enforcement: No international enforcement mechanism; unilateral SAF action
new_developments.asymmetric: The economic warfare dimension is absent from most coverage focused on territorial gains. Gold mine targeting signals strategic shift to revenue degradation, potentially affecting UAE illicit finance networks.

module_9.friction_analysis: Economic coercion friction is highest in transatlantic divergence on Russia sanctions (EU 20th package vs US oil waiver) and Sudan economic warfare introduction (gold mine strikes). The EU-US contradiction undermines sanctions coherence and signals weakening coordination. Sudan gold mine strikes represent novel economic warfare dimension in a conflict previously characterised by purely kinetic operations. Both developments indicate structural shifts in economic coercion strategy rather than episodic adjustments.
module_9.arms_control_legal: New START remains suspended with no successor negotiations active. NPT compliance contested for DPRK (withdrew 2003) and Iran (safeguards verification absent). Russia November 2024 doctrine shift occurred in verification vacuum. No functioning arms control architecture between major nuclear powers. International humanitarian law under pressure in Ukraine (ceasefire-adjacent civilian targeting), Gaza (ceasefire violations), Sudan (displacement), and Lebanon (ceasefire unraveling). No active ICC or ICJ proceedings this week.

sanctions_highlights.title: EU 20th sanctions package against Russia
sanctions_highlights.summary: Adopted 23 April 2026. Scope and specific designations not detailed in source material. Undermined by simultaneous US oil waiver extension.

international_frameworks.summary: No change this week. DPRK nuclear acceleration continues (7 tests 2026 to April). Iran nuclear programme at impasse. NPT compliance verification relies on IAEA safeguards, which are absent for DPRK (withdrew 2003) and contested for Iran. Treaty architecture under structural stress from non-compliance without enforcement.
international_frameworks.asymmetric: NPT structural stress is underweighted in coverage that treats DPRK and Iran as discrete proliferation challenges. The absence of verification frameworks for both actors creates compounding uncertainty.

international_frameworks.summary: No change this week. Russia suspended participation February 2023. No successor negotiations active. Absence of strategic arms control framework leaves no verification mechanism for nuclear posture changes. Russia November 2024 doctrine shift occurred in verification vacuum.
international_frameworks.asymmetric: The verification vacuum is underweighted in coverage that treats Russia doctrine shift as a discrete event. The absence of New START means no external transparency on Russian nuclear posture changes.

crisis_management_mechanisms.summary: Trump-brokered Victory Day ceasefire (9-11 May) collapsed within 24 hours of expiry. This is the second consecutive US-brokered ceasefire to collapse immediately (Easter + Victory Day), demonstrating structural fragility of bilateral arrangements without multilateral enforcement. No framework for sustained negotiations produced.
At impasse. Iran 27 April decouple proposal (separate nuclear from regional issues) rejected by US. No active verification framework. No pathway to de-escalation visible.

crisis_management_mechanisms.item: Sudan Quad mediation (US, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt)
crisis_management_mechanisms.summary: Collapsed. No active diplomatic framework for year-four war. Regional actors have no coordinated response to spillover risk.

ceasefire_negotiations.summary: Collapsed within 24 hours of 11 May expiry. Russia resumed strikes immediately. No structural diplomatic progress. Second consecutive US-brokered ceasefire to fail immediately.
Holding nominally but daily violations continue. Hamas rejected disarmament. Phase 2 negotiations stalled. No enforcement mechanism for violations.
Unraveling. Nominal only as of 26-27 April. Israeli strikes intensified across southern Lebanon to Litani River on 21 April. New evacuation orders issued. I6 displacement at RED threshold.
April 2026 talks produced protocols but ground fighting continues. No enforcement mechanism. Regional actors (Rwanda, Uganda) not bound.

stability_gaps.gap: Absence of multilateral crisis management frameworks
stability_gaps.analysis: No functioning multilateral frameworks across all major theatres. US bilateral arrangements structurally fragile. Regional mechanisms (ASEAN, AU, OSCE) ineffective. No active arms control negotiations between major powers.
stability_gaps.asymmetric: If unaddressed, the absence of multilateral frameworks means no institutional resilience when bilateral arrangements fail. Crisis management capacity deficit is structural, not episodic.

stability_gaps.analysis: New START suspended. NPT compliance contested for DPRK and Iran. Russia doctrine shift occurred without external verification. No transparency on nuclear posture changes for major powers.
stability_gaps.asymmetric: If unaddressed, nuclear threshold pressure rises in transparency vacuum. Compounding uncertainty from DPRK, Russia, and Iran nuclear postures creates systemic risk.

items.title: Russia deadliest civilian strike wave 2026: 73 killed, 400+ injured in four days
items.accountability_friction: Ceasefire-adjacent civilian targeting pattern (Easter + Victory Day) demonstrates deliberate use of civilian harm as pre-ceasefire bargaining chip. IHL accountability unclear: no active ICC proceedings on Ukraine civilian targeting. Pattern is structural, not episodic.
items.asymmetric: The structural use of civilian harm as a pre-ceasefire bargaining chip is underweighted in coverage that treats ceasefires as de-escalation signals. IHL accountability gap: no enforcement mechanism for ceasefire-adjacent escalation.

items.title: Sudan displacement surges to 4 million+ refugees with new Blue Nile and Kordofan waves
items.accountability_friction: Multi-front expansion (Blue Nile + Kordofan) drives new displacement waves. Agricultural and water infrastructure at risk. IHL accountability unclear: no active ICC proceedings on Sudan displacement. Regional spillover into Chad and South Sudan threatens humanitarian catastrophe.
items.asymmetric: The agricultural and water infrastructure risk is underweighted in coverage focused on territorial gains. Displacement velocity threatens famine conditions in year-four war.

items.title: Israel-Lebanon I6 displacement at RED threshold; new evacuation orders issued
items.accountability_friction: Ceasefire unraveling with new evacuation orders confirms worsening. I6 displacement extreme. IHL accountability unclear: no active ICC proceedings on Lebanon displacement. UNIFIL ineffective as ceasefire enforcement mechanism.
items.asymmetric: The ceasefire unraveling is underweighted in coverage that treats November 2024 ceasefire as holding. I6 RED threshold indicates humanitarian catastrophe risk.

module_12.title: Hybrid Warfare and Cognitive Dimensions

items.title: Medvedev publishes European drone manufacturer target list as hybrid FIMI and coercion operation
items.summary: Dmitry Medvedev on 15 April published a list of European drone manufacturers designated as potential targets for Russian Armed Forces. This represents a hybrid FIMI and coercion operation, explicitly threatening civilian industrial targets in NATO member states. The publication creates a legal and operational predicate for future strikes on European soil framed as counter-force operations.
items.asymmetric: The hybrid nature of the operation is underweighted in coverage that treats it as rhetoric only. The operational preparation dimension (target designation) creates legal and operational predicate for future strikes.

items.title: Russian pre-ceasefire escalation likely accompanied by information operations framing strikes as Ukrainian provocation
items.summary: The pattern of ceasefire-adjacent civilian targeting (Easter + Victory Day) is likely accompanied by Russian information operations framing strikes as Ukrainian provocation or justifying them as pre-emptive defence. This is a structural FIMI pattern tied to diplomatic windows.
items.asymmetric: The FIMI dimension of ceasefire-adjacent escalation is absent from most coverage. Russian information operations likely frame strikes as defensive, undermining Western narratives of Russian aggression.

f_flag_watch.description: Ceasefire fragile with daily violations and no Phase 2 framework. Hamas rejection of disarmament unverified. F4 flag maintained per reasoner analysis.
Switzerland talks produced protocols but fighting continues. F4 flag maintained per reasoner analysis. Talks interim (AMBER, not RED expulsion).
Impasse confirmed; no verification of decouple proposal progress. F4 flag maintained per reasoner analysis. Impasse is AMBER-equivalent (talks stalled, not expelled).
No new data contradicting limited framing inconsistency; Op Eternal Darkness scale persists. F4 flag maintained per reasoner analysis.
Ongoing Hormuz closure and Gulf attacks contradict dismantled claim. F3 flag maintained per reasoner analysis.

asymmetric_flags: Medvedev target list publication is hybrid FIMI and coercion operation — flag for FCW monitor
Russian pre-ceasefire escalation likely accompanied by information operations framing strikes as Ukrainian provocation — flag for FCW monitor

module_13.title: Civil Society, Displacement, and Conflict-Societal Interface

items.title: Sudan displacement surges to 4 million+ refugees with new Blue Nile and Kordofan waves
items.summary: Multi-front expansion (Blue Nile + Kordofan) drives new displacement waves. Agricultural and water infrastructure at risk. Regional spillover into Chad and South Sudan threatens humanitarian catastrophe. Displacement velocity threatens famine conditions in year-four war.
items.asymmetric: The agricultural and water infrastructure risk is underweighted in coverage focused on territorial gains. Displacement velocity has structural implications for post-conflict settlement prospects.

items.title: Israel-Lebanon I6 displacement at RED threshold; new evacuation orders issued
items.summary: Ceasefire unraveling with new evacuation orders confirms worsening. I6 displacement extreme. Humanitarian catastrophe risk. UNIFIL ineffective as ceasefire enforcement mechanism.
items.asymmetric: The ceasefire unraveling is underweighted in coverage that treats November 2024 ceasefire as holding. I6 RED threshold indicates humanitarian catastrophe risk with long-term societal implications.

structural_trends.title: Ceasefire-adjacent displacement spikes
structural_trends.trend: Pattern of displacement spikes tied to diplomatic windows (ceasefires) emerging across Ukraine and Lebanon. Signals that ceasefires function as operational breathing space for actors to maximise civilian pressure before temporary pauses, not genuine de-escalation opportunities.

asymmetric_flags: Displacement velocity in Sudan threatens famine conditions and has structural implications for post-conflict settlement prospects
Ceasefire-adjacent displacement spikes in Ukraine and Lebanon signal structural pattern, not episodic events

module_14.title: Power Structures and Alliance Dynamics

items.title: Transatlantic divergence on Russia sanctions undermines EU coherence
items.summary: The EU adopted its 20th sanctions package against Russia on 23 April while the US extended Russian oil sanctions relief for 30 days, creating contradictory Western posture. This undermines EU sanctions coherence and signals transatlantic divergence on economic coercion strategy. US unilateral ceasefire brokering without European involvement signals continued marginalisation of European diplomatic agency in Ukraine settlement process.
items.asymmetric: The marginalisation of European diplomatic agency is underweighted in coverage that treats US-brokered ceasefires as positive signals. The structural fragility of these arrangements indicates that NATO cohesion on Ukraine is weakening, not strengthening.

items.title: US asset redeployment to Middle East reduces deterrence posture on Korean Peninsula
items.summary: US asset redeployment to Middle East creates permissive environment for DPRK nuclear testing surge (7 tests January to April 2026). Signals prioritisation of Iran theatre over Korean Peninsula, creating strategic opportunity for DPRK.
items.asymmetric: The permissive environment created by US asset redeployment is underweighted in coverage focused on DPRK testing alone. The redeployment signals strategic prioritisation that has structural implications for Indo-Pacific deterrence.

domains.note: Russia deadliest civilian strike wave 2026 demonstrates sustained conventional firepower concentration. Sudan SAF multi-front expansion demonstrates regional concentration.
DPRK nuclear acceleration (7 tests 2026) and Russia doctrine shift (November 2024) demonstrate nuclear arsenal concentration among major powers. Iran nuclear programme at impasse but unverified.
No material change in C2 architecture concentration this week.
No material change in intelligence capacity concentration this week.

Transatlantic divergence on Russia sanctions (EU 20th package vs US oil waiver) signals dispersing economic coercion capacity. Sudan SAF gold mine strikes introduce new regional actor (SAF) to economic warfare domain.
US unilateral ceasefire brokering without European involvement signals diplomatic influence concentration. Marginalisation of European diplomatic agency in Ukraine settlement process.

asymmetric_flags: Transatlantic divergence on Russia sanctions signals weakening NATO cohesion, not strengthening
US asset redeployment to Middle East creates permissive environment for DPRK nuclear acceleration

module_15.title: Leadership and Command Personnel

leadership_signals.observation: Shoigu Article 51 warning to Finland and Baltic states
leadership_signals.detail: Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu on 16 April warned Finland and Baltic countries that permitting Ukraine to use their airspace for operations on Russian soil would make them accomplices to aggression, potentially giving Moscow grounds to invoke Article 51 of the UN Charter on the right to self-defence.
leadership_signals.12_month_implication: Creates legal predicate for future kinetic action against NATO members that could be framed as defensive rather than offensive. If sustained, could fracture NATO consensus on Article 5 response to Russian action against Baltic states or Finland.

leadership_signals.detail: Dmitry Medvedev on 15 April published a list of European drone manufacturers designated as potential targets for Russian Armed Forces.
leadership_signals.12_month_implication: Creates operational predicate for future strikes on European civilian industrial targets framed as counter-force operations. If sustained, could escalate to kinetic action against NATO member industrial infrastructure.

asymmetric_flags: Shoigu Article 51 framing creates legal predicate for future kinetic action against NATO members
Medvedev target list publication creates operational predicate for strikes on European civilian industrial targets

stability_health_composite.note: Stability health composite deteriorating across all components. Diplomatic channel health at structural low (0.20) due to collapse of US-brokered ceasefires, Iran nuclear impasse, and Sudan Quad mediation failure. Arms control coverage remains weak (0.25) with New START suspended and NPT compliance contested. Escalation risk aggregate elevated (0.40 inverted = 0.60 risk) due to Sudan multi-front expansion, Russia ceasefire-adjacent escalation, and DPRK nuclear acceleration. Alliance cohesion weakening (0.40) due to transatlantic divergence on Russia sanctions and marginalisation of European diplomatic agency.

key_judgments.judgment: The pattern of ceasefire-adjacent civilian targeting in Ukraine (Easter + Victory Day) represents a structural Russian strategy of using civilian harm as a pre-ceasefire bargaining chip, not episodic violence. This pattern is now in its second documented cycle and approaching structural threshold.

US-brokered ceasefires in Ukraine lack the institutional architecture to sustain de-escalation. The structural fragility of bilateral arrangements without multilateral enforcement is demonstrated by two consecutive collapses within 24 hours (Easter + Victory Day). These arrangements function as operational breathing space for Russia rather than genuine diplomatic openings.

Sudan Armed Forces multi-front expansion (Blue Nile + Kordofan airstrikes + gold mine targeting) represents a structural shift from year-long stalemate to multi-domain offensive operations. The Blue Nile front opens a potential north-south partition scenario that could create a permanent failed-state corridor from Khartoum to the Ethiopian border.

The introduction of economic warfare targeting (gold mines) in Sudan signals a shift from purely kinetic to hybrid strategy. Gold mine strikes aim to degrade RSF revenue streams via UAE networks, potentially reducing illicit gold flows. This represents a novel dimension in year-four war with implications for post-conflict settlement.

Shoigu Article 51 warning to Finland and Baltic states represents a novel escalation in legal framing against NATO members. Invoking the UN Charter self-defence provision creates a legal predicate for future kinetic action that could be framed as defensive rather than offensive. This is a structural shift in Russian legal positioning, not episodic rhetoric.

The dual-chokepoint risk (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb) created by Houthi Red Sea intervention warnings threatens 30 percent of global seaborne oil trade and 10 percent of all maritime commerce. Simultaneous closure of both straits would create a systemic energy and commodities shock. This risk is underweighted in coverage that treats Iran conflict and Yemen as separate theatres.

Transatlantic divergence on Russia sanctions (EU 20th package vs US oil waiver extension) undermines EU sanctions coherence and signals weakening NATO cohesion on economic coercion strategy. The marginalisation of European diplomatic agency in Ukraine settlement process (US unilateral ceasefire brokering) indicates that NATO cohesion is deteriorating, not strengthening.

DPRK nuclear acceleration (7 tests January to April 2026) occurs in a permissive environment created by US asset redeployment to Middle East. This signals US prioritisation of Iran theatre over Korean Peninsula, creating strategic opportunity for DPRK. Warrants Worsening trajectory.

open_findings.why_it_matters: Signals US prioritisation of Iran theatre over Korean Peninsula, creating strategic opportunity for DPRK; warrants Worsening trajectory

open_findings.headline: Medvedev published list of European drone manufacturers designated as potential targets for Russian Armed Forces
open_findings.why_it_matters: Hybrid FIMI and coercion operation explicitly threatening civilian industrial targets in NATO member states; creates operational predicate for future strikes

open_findings.headline: AQAP political violence hit highest level since November 2022 using drones and IEDs against STC affiliates in Yemen
open_findings.why_it_matters: Drone use signals AQAP reassertion amid Houthi tensions; possible external support linking to broader Iran proxy dynamics

proposed_patches.material_change_justification: Five new escalation events documented this cycle: Russia deadliest civilian strike wave 2026, Sudan SAF Blue Nile offensive, Sudan SAF gold mine strikes, Shoigu Article 51 warning, Houthi Red Sea intervention warning. All meet strategic significance threshold.

new_value.event: Russia conducted deadliest civilian strike wave of 2026, killing 73 civilians and injuring over 400 across Zaporizhia, Kramatorsk, Dnipro, Merefa, and Sumy in four-day pre-ceasefire campaign (4-8 May)
new_value.strategic_significance: Demonstrates structural pattern of ceasefire-adjacent civilian targeting as pre-ceasefire bargaining chip; second documented cycle (Easter + Victory Day) approaching structural threshold

new_value.event: Sudan Armed Forces captured Damazin in Blue Nile state, opening new front threatening north-south partition
new_value.strategic_significance: Structural shift from year-long stalemate to multi-domain offensive operations; opens potential permanent failed-state corridor from Khartoum to Ethiopian border

new_value.event: Sudan Armed Forces conducted manned airstrikes on RSF-controlled gold mines in Kordofan as economic warfare
new_value.strategic_significance: Introduction of economic warfare targeting signals shift from purely kinetic to hybrid strategy; aims to degrade RSF revenue streams via UAE networks

new_value.event: Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu warned Finland and Baltic states over alleged Ukrainian use of their airspace, invoking Article 51 of UN Charter
new_value.strategic_significance: Novel escalation in legal framing against NATO members; creates legal predicate for future kinetic action that could be framed as defensive rather than offensive

new_value.event: Houthis issued military intervention warning threatening Red Sea chokepoint disruption if Iran attacked
new_value.strategic_significance: Creates dual-chokepoint risk (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb) threatening 30 percent of global seaborne oil trade and 10 percent of all maritime commerce

proposed_patches.rule_applied: Rule 30 — f_flag_history additive log with status updates
proposed_patches.material_change_justification: No new F-flags applied this cycle. All existing F4 and F3 flags maintained per reasoner analysis. No status changes to Resolved.

new_value.detail: Ceasefire fragile with daily violations and no Phase 2 framework; Hamas rejection of disarmament unverified
Switzerland talks produced protocols but fighting continues; talks interim (AMBER, not RED expulsion)
Impasse confirmed; no verification of decouple proposal progress; impasse is AMBER-equivalent (talks stalled, not expelled)
No new data contradicting limited framing inconsistency; Op Eternal Darkness scale persists
Ongoing Hormuz closure and Gulf attacks contradict dismantled claim

proposed_patches.material_change_justification: Material updates to mediation architecture for Russia-Ukraine (US bilateral ceasefire collapsed), US-Israel-Iran (nuclear talks at impasse, decouple proposal rejected), Sudan (Quad mediation collapsed), and DRC (Switzerland talks produced protocols but fighting continues).

new_value.un_security_council: Deadlocked — Russia veto power blocks substantive resolutions; no active UNSC mediation framework
Deadlocked — US veto blocks ceasefire enforcement resolutions; humanitarian resolutions pass but lack enforcement
No active UNSC mediation; UNIFIL mandate renewal pending

frameworks: Nuclear talks (at impasse; decouple proposal rejected 27 April 2026)
new_value.un_security_council: Deadlocked — no UNSC role in nuclear talks; regional mediation absent
No active UNSC mediation; Quad mediation (US, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt) collapsed

frameworks: Switzerland talks (April 2026 protocols produced but fighting continues)
new_value.un_security_council: No active UNSC mediation; regional actors (Rwanda, Uganda) not bound by protocols
Deadlocked — China and Russia block substantive resolutions

proposed_patches.rule_applied: Rule 32 — treaty_posture list of treaties relevant to active conflicts
proposed_patches.material_change_justification: No material treaty status changes this cycle. New START remains suspended. NPT compliance contested for DPRK (7 tests 2026) and Iran (nuclear impasse). CTBT not in force but DPRK tests violate norms.

new_value.details: Russia suspended participation February 2023; no successor negotiations active; absence of verification framework leaves no transparency on nuclear posture changes
DPRK nuclear acceleration continues (7 tests 2026 to April); Iran nuclear programme at impasse; NPT compliance verification relies on IAEA safeguards, absent for DPRK and contested for Iran
DPRK not a signatory; 7 tests 2026 to April violate CTBT norms but treaty not in force; no enforcement mechanism
